name: Swedish Cinnamon Buns
steps: Mix the flour, half the butter, milk, and cardamom into a smooth dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Roll out, spread with the remaining butter, roll up, and slice into buns.
Bake at 375F for 15-18 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, half the butter, milk, and half the cardamom into a smooth, slightly soft dough. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Roll the dough into a rectangle and spread with the remaining softened butter.
Mix the brown sugar, cinnamon, and remaining cardamom, and sprinkle evenly over the buttered dough.
Roll up tightly from the long side and slice into rounds, placing them cut-side up on a baking sheet.
Brush with the egg wash and bake at 375F (190C) for 15-20 minutes until golden - Sweden's beloved kanelbulle, with cardamom as the defining flavor note.
description: Kanelbullar, cinnamon buns, are so central to Swedish culture that Sweden has an entire holiday, Kanelbullens dag, dedicated to them.

name: Swedish Filmjolk-Style Yogurt Bowl

ingredients.ingredient: Chobani Plain Low-Fat Greek Yogurt, Plain
Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Spoon the yogurt into a bowl.
Top with the oats and cranberry sauce.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chobani Plain Low-Fat Greek Yogurt, Plain
Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Toast the oats in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Spoon the yogurt (filmjolk, a tangy Swedish cultured milk, is the traditional base) into a bowl.
Top with the toasted oats, cranberry sauce (standing in for lingonberry), and a drizzle of honey.
description: Filmjölk, a tangy, thick soured milk product, is a distinctly Swedish breakfast staple - regular yogurt stands in here, since filmjölk itself isn't a distinct item in this database, though it has its own specific, milder tang.

name: Swedish Open-Faced Sandwich
steps: Top the bread with the cheese and sliced egg.
Serve open-faced.
steps_detailed: Layer the rye crispbread with the sliced cheese and hard-boiled egg.
Top with the diced tomatoes and a scattering of fresh dill.
Serve open-faced - the smorgasbord (smorgasbord literally means 'bread and butter table') tradition of open sandwiches is a Swedish breakfast and lunch staple.
description: Smörgås, open-faced sandwiches, a Swedish everyday staple.

name: Swedish Meatballs
steps: Mix the beef with the milk and a little flour, and shape into small meatballs.
Brown the meatballs, then remove.
Whisk more flour into the broth and simmer into a gravy, then return the meatballs to warm through.

ingredients_full.note: as a lingonberry stand-in, to serve

steps_detailed: Mix the beef with the milk, half the flour, egg, and onion, and shape into small, evenly-sized meatballs.
Brown the meatballs in the butter over medium heat, 6-8 minutes, turning to color all sides, then remove and set aside.
Whisk the remaining flour into the pan drippings, cook 1 minute, then gradually whisk in the beef broth, simmering 4-5 minutes until thickened into a gravy.
Return the meatballs to the gravy and simmer 5 minutes to finish cooking through.
Serve with the cranberry sauce alongside, standing in for lingonberry jam - Sweden's most famous culinary export.
description: Köttbullar, Swedish meatballs served with a cream gravy and lingonberry, are one of the country's most famous culinary exports.

name: Swedish Pea Soup
steps: Saute the bacon and onion together until fragrant.
Add the peas and broth, and simmer 15-18 minutes until the peas are tender.
Blend partially for a rustic, thick texture.
steps_detailed: Saute the diced bacon and onion together for 5-6 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden.
Add the peas, garlic, broth, and allspice, and simmer 18-20 minutes until the peas are very tender.
description: Blend partially with an immersion blender, leaving some texture - traditionally eaten on Thursdays in Sweden, often followed by pancakes for dessert.

name: Swedish Toast Skagen
steps: Mix the chopped shrimp with the sour cream and dill.
Toast the bread and top generously with the shrimp mixture.
Serve immediately.
steps_detailed: Mix the chopped shrimp with the sour cream, dill, lime juice, and black pepper until well combined.
Toast the bread until golden and crisp.
Top generously with the shrimp mixture and serve immediately - a beloved Swedish open-faced sandwich, especially popular as a light lunch or starter.
description: Toast Skagen, shrimp in a creamy dressing over toast, is a beloved Swedish appetizer named for the Danish town, despite being a Swedish invention.

name: Swedish Sillsallad

ingredients.note: well-salted, as a pickled herring stand-in

steps: Salt the fish generously and poach until just cooked, then dice.
Combine the diced fish, beets, and potato with the sour cream.
Chill before serving.

ingredients_full.note: well-salted, as a pickled herring stand-in

steps_detailed: Salt the fish generously and let sit 15 minutes to firm up, mimicking the tang of true pickled herring, then poach gently until just cooked and dice.
Combine the diced fish, beets, potato, and onion in a bowl.
Fold in the sour cream and vinegar, and season with salt.
Chill at least 30 minutes, then scatter with fresh dill before serving - a classic Swedish Christmas table salad, valued for its striking pink color from the beets.
description: Sillsallad, a beet and herring salad, a classic Swedish Christmas dish.

name: Swedish Salmon Gravlax-Style
steps: Coat the salmon generously with salt, sugar, and the chopped dill.
Wrap tightly and cure in the fridge at least 4-6 hours, or overnight for best flavor.
Slice thin and serve, traditionally with a mustard-dill sauce.
steps_detailed: Combine a generous amount of salt with the sugar, black pepper, and chopped dill.
Coat the salmon thoroughly with the mixture, pressing it into the flesh.
Wrap tightly in plastic wrap and place in the fridge with a light weight on top, curing at least 6-8 hours, or up to 2 days for a firmer texture.
description: Rinse off the cure, pat dry, and slice very thin on the diagonal - gravlax is one of Sweden's most iconic dishes, traditionally served with a sweet mustard-dill sauce.

name: Swedish Pytt i Panna
steps: Fry the potato until golden and crisp.
Brown the beef and onion separately, then combine everything in the pan.
Top with a fried egg before serving.
steps_detailed: Fry the diced potato in half the oil over medium heat, 12-15 minutes, until golden and crisp on the outside, tender inside.
In a separate pan, brown the beef and onion with the black pepper until well cooked, 8-10 minutes.
Combine the potato, beef, and onion together in one pan and toss to combine, frying together 2-3 more minutes.
Top each portion with a fried egg - a beloved Swedish way to use up leftovers, named literally 'small pieces in a pan.'
description: Pyttipanna, diced meat, potato, and onion fried together, a classic Swedish way to use up leftovers.

name: Swedish Fish Casserole (Janssons Frestelse)
steps: Layer the potato, fish, and onion in a small baking dish.
Pour the milk over the top.
Bake at 375F for 40-45 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the sliced potato, fish, and onion in a small buttered baking dish, seasoning with black pepper between layers.
Pour the milk over the top and dot with the remaining butter.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 40-50 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden and bubbling - a beloved, richly savory Swedish casserole, despite the debated origin of its name.
description: Salt the fish generously to mimic the salty, umami-rich pickled sprats traditionally used, and slice thin.

name: Swedish Kalops

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the carrot and broth, and simmer, covered, 1.5-2 hours until the beef is very tender.
Serve with boiled potatoes and cranberry sauce.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Dust the beef lightly in the flour and brown it with the onion over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the carrot, beef broth, bay leaf, and allspice.
Cover and simmer over low heat 1.5-2 hours until the beef is completely tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Remove the bay leaf and serve with boiled potatoes and cranberry sauce (standing in for lingonberry) - a comforting, everyday Swedish beef stew, distinctively spiced with allspice.
description: Kalops, a slow-cooked beef stew with allspice, a Swedish home-cooking staple.

name: Swedish Knackebrod with Cheese
steps: Top the crispbread with the sliced cheese and a spoonful of cranberry sauce.
Serve as a simple snack.
steps_detailed: Spread the crispbread lightly with butter.
Top with the sliced cheese and a spoonful of cranberry sauce (standing in for lingonberry preserves) - a classic, simple Swedish snack pairing.
description: Knäckebröd, crisp rye crackerbread, a Swedish pantry staple.

name: Swedish Shrimp Sandwich (Rakmacka)
steps: Mix the shrimp with the sour cream.
Pile onto the bread and top with the sliced egg.
Serve open-faced.
steps_detailed: Mix the shrimp with the sour cream and dill until well combined.
Pile generously onto the bread, top with the sliced egg and diced tomatoes.
Serve open-faced - a beloved Swedish sandwich, especially popular in summer.
description: Räkmacka, an open-faced shrimp sandwich, a beloved Swedish lunch.

name: Swedish Almond Cookies
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then mix in the flour and cardamom.
Shape into small rounds and press flat.
Bake at 350F for 10-12 minutes until just golden at the edges.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar together until light, then beat in the vanilla.
Mix in the flour and cardamom until a soft dough forms.
Shape into small rounds, press flat with a fork, and place on a baking sheet.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 10-14 minutes until just golden at the edges - a simple, buttery Swedish fika (coffee break) treat.
description: Simple almond cookies, a common Swedish sweet.

name: Swedish Pressgurka
steps: Thinly slice the cucumber and toss with the vinegar, sugar, and dill.
Let sit at least 15 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Thinly slice the cucumber as evenly as possible, ideally with a mandoline for paper-thin rounds.
Whisk the vinegar, sugar, and black pepper together until the sugar dissolves, then toss with the cucumber and dill.
Let sit at room temperature at least 20-30 minutes, or chill for a few hours, so the cucumber lightly pickles - a bright, tangy side found at almost every Swedish meal.
description: Pressgurka, a simple pressed cucumber salad, a Swedish staple side, especially alongside meatballs.

name: Swedish Princess Cake Style Sponge

ingredients.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

steps: Beat the eggs and sugar until pale, then fold in the flour to make a simple sponge batter.
Bake at 350F for 18-20 minutes until springy, then cool.
Top with the whipped cream.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped with a little sugar

steps_detailed: Beat the eggs and sugar together until pale and thickened, about 4-5 minutes.
Sift the flour and baking powder together and fold in gently.
Bake in a small buttered pan at 350F (175C) for 18-22 minutes until springy and golden. Let cool completely.
Slice the sponge horizontally and spread the jam over the bottom layer, then the whipped cream, and replace the top.
description: A simplified nod to Sweden's iconic prinsesstarta, traditionally domed and covered in green marzipan.

name: Swedish Semlor
steps: Mix the flour, butter, milk, and cardamom into a soft dough, and bake small rounds at 375F for 15 minutes.
Once cool, cut a lid off the top, hollow out a little, and fill with whipped cream.
Replace the lid and dust with powdered sugar.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

steps_detailed: Mix the flour, butter, milk, cardamom, and sugar into a soft, slightly enriched dough. Rest 20-30 minutes.
Shape into small round buns, brush with the egg wash, and bake at 375F (190C) for 15-18 minutes until golden. Let cool completely.
Cut a lid off the top of each bun and hollow out a little of the crumb from the center (save the crumbs).
Mix some of the reserved crumbs with a spoonful of the whipped cream and spoon back into the hollow, then top with more whipped cream.
description: Replace the lid and dust with powdered or granulated sugar - Sweden's beloved cardamom bun, traditionally eaten before Lent.

name: Swedish Chokladbollar

ingredients.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Mix the oats, cocoa powder, softened butter, and sugar with a splash of cold coffee or water into a firm, moldable mixture.
Roll into small balls.
Chill until firm, about 30 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Soften the butter and mix with the sugar, cocoa powder, and vanilla until well combined.
Stir in the oats and a splash of cold coffee (traditional, for depth of flavor) or water, mixing until the mixture holds together when pressed.
Roll into small balls, about 1 inch across.
Roll each ball in the shredded coconut to coat.
Chill at least 30-40 minutes until firm - a beloved no-bake Swedish treat, found in every bakery and home kitchen.
description: Chokladbollar, no-bake chocolate-oat balls rolled in pearl sugar or coconut, one of Sweden's most beloved simple treats.

name: Swedish Mustard-Dill Sauce
steps: Whisk the mustard, honey, dill, and oil together until smooth.
Serve alongside gravlax or other cured fish.
steps_detailed: Whisk the mustard, honey, and vinegar together until smooth, then slowly whisk in the oil until emulsified.
Stir in the chopped dill.
Serve alongside gravlax, cured salmon, or boiled potatoes - the classic Swedish hovmastarsas that always accompanies gravlax.
description: Hovmästarsås, a mustard-dill sauce, the classic pairing for Swedish gravlax.

name: Swedish Lingonberry Sauce
steps: Simmer the fresh cranberries with the sugar and canned cranberry sauce for 8-10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Cool before serving alongside meatballs or roasted meats.
steps_detailed: Combine the fresh cranberries, sugar, canned cranberry sauce, and cinnamon with a splash of water in a small saucepan.
Simmer over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until the fresh berries have mostly broken down and the mixture has thickened.
Cool before serving - a reasonable stand-in for lingonberry sauce, the tart-sweet condiment inseparable from Swedish meatballs.
description: Lingonberry sauce is the classic accompaniment to Swedish meatballs, with a distinctive tartness that's milder and more floral than cranberry - real lingonberries aren't a distinct item in this database, so cranberries stand in here, giving a similar tart-sweet balance but a sharper flavor than the real thing.
